Transcrição Completa: Your Personal Life Impacts Your Professional Life

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "21 Your Personal Life Impacts Your Professional Life"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Título mostrando conexão entre vida pessoal e profissional

[00:02] Your personal life affects your professional life. There's no doubt about it. I want to be clear. There's a lot of things in life that happens that simply put we can't control.

[00:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Uncontrollable Life Issues"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Lista de problemas pessoais: família, médicos, pets, filhos, relacionamentos

[00:15] Family issues, medical issues, maybe you have an issue with your pet. Maybe something happened with one of your kids. Maybe you broke up with somebody and you're in a tough spot mentally. All those things are going to affect your professional life.

[00:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Brandon Carter's Tracking System"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Sistema de tracking: humor diário + macros (carbs, protein, fats)

[00:35] One of my clients, his name is Brandon Carter. Brandon tracks his salespeople's daily moods and he also tracks their macros, carbs, protein, fats. He's King Keto on Instagram if you want to look him up so you can see how physically fit he is.

[00:50] But point is Brandon tracks his salespeople's moods and their macros.a

[00:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Transparency Opportunity Required"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Canal aberto para vendedores reportarem problemas pessoais

[01:00] You have to have some type of opportunity for your salespeople to come to you transparently and acknowledge when they're having some type of issue in their personal life.

[01:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Jeremy's Mercedes Gift Story"

NÚMEROS: 300% sales increase in one month

VISUAL: Mini Cooper problemático → Mercedes Benz = resultado dramático

[01:15] So, as an example, in my sales organization, one of my sales guys, he was having an issue with his car. He had this piece of shit Mini Cooper and simply put, it was having all these issues.

[01:25] What I did is for Christmas, I got him a new Mercedes Benz. He's very happy with it and it's drastically improved. I mean, literally that one month, he had an additional like 300% in sales in comparison to what he had just that month prior.

[01:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Sustained Struggling = Personal Issues"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Vendedor com performance baixa por período prolongado

[01:45] So, when you see sales people kind of struggling for a sustained period of time, it's likely because some type of personal issue.

[01:55] Now, there's not a lot that we can do if something is drastically out of our control, like a family medical issue, a personal medical issue, something that happened with like a death.

[02:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Money-Related Solutions"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Problemas solucionáveis: mudança, contratação de mudanceiros

[02:12] But there's a lot that we can do, especially for things that just relate to money. Maybe somebody's in the middle of moving. We can help encourage them how to expedite a move through having movers come in in comparison to them doing everything themselves.

[02:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Environmental Productivity Triggers"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Ambiente de trabalho estabelecido vs novo ambiente

[02:30] Letting the salesperson know that, okay, you just moved to a new location. All your productivity habits that you previously had established were really largely due to environmental triggers that caused you to be productive.

[02:42] When you walk into your office in your home, there's a level of productivity that naturally happens there because that's where you work. Versus when you're in a new environment, all those existing productivity habits are uprooted by being in a new environment.

[02:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "David Meltzer's Approach"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: David Meltzer, ex-CEO Samsung Mobile, sports marketing agency California

[03:05] Teaching people things like this. I had a mentor, his name is David Meltzer, and David runs a huge organization out in California. It's a sports marketing agency. Works with a lot of the professional franchises out there in the sports world.

[03:18] In addition to that, he used to be the CEO of Samsung Mobile at one point in the US. David is incredible in terms of how he operates as a business person.

[03:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Friday Personal Development Sessions"

NÚMEROS: Every Friday

VISUAL: Sessões não-business: meditação, espiritualidade, vida pessoal

[03:32] Every Friday he does a totally non-business training, just a personal development training session on Fridays. He'll go over a wide range of stuff from meditation to spiritual views to just asking people questions about how their personal lives are.

[03:48] Him and his staff have an extremely transparent relationship with one another about what's going on in their personal lives. And that gives David an opportunity to step in and help them in those areas if they're ever having issues there.

[04:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Advice for Salespeople"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Transparência com chefe, tirar dias off para se recuperar

[04:08] Because once again, your personal life will affect your professional life in every way, shape, or form. If you as a salesperson are sitting here watching this right now, just be transparent with your boss.

[04:18] A lot of people are pretty cool about, dude, something's going on in your personal life. Why don't you take a couple days off so you can recoup, deal with it, and then come back into the business so you can laser focus on the professional side.

[04:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Waste of Leads and Money"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Calendário cheio de oportunidades sendo desperdiçadas por fadiga mental

[04:35] At the end of the day, it's a big waste of leads, and I hate to say it like this, but it is just an overall big waste of money for the business to fill up a salesperson's calendar with a bunch of opportunities when they're having a bunch of mental fatigue and personal life issues.

[04:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Management Responsibility"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Gestão influenciando vida pessoal para sustentar performance profissional

[04:55] Those leads are essentially going to be wasted because that person's in a bad mental position. So it's definitely a huge responsibility of the sales management to not control our salespeople's personal lives but to help make sure that we influence it to be as sustainable and consistent and thriving as much as possible because it absolutely affects the amount of money they're going to bring in for the business professionally.

🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy revela uma VERDADE BRUTAL que gestores medíocres ignoram! Enquanto empresas fracas fingem que problemas pessoais não existem, Jeremy INVESTE na vida completa dos vendedores. A história do Mercedes é DEVASTADORA - $40K+ investimento gerando 300% de aumento em vendas. Jeremy entende que vendedores são SERES HUMANOS, não máquinas. O exemplo do David Meltzer é GENIAL - sessões de desenvolvimento pessoal às sextas. Jeremy não gerencia apenas performance, ele OTIMIZA vidas inteiras!
